1989_09_26-India-Mumbai-Real-Opulence-is-Renunciation_SB-7-11-16-st3-vr01

Meta Data v9

Event Name/Title

Punch Your Way Back Home Back To Godhead. Real Opulence is Renunciation

Event Date

26 Sep 1989

Event Location

India, Mumbai

Event Type

Temple - SB class

Transcriber

Kumaresan Sundaram, nayanasundri devidasi

Completed

07 Jul 2021

Verse

SB 7.11.16

Tags – Places

Tags – Persons

Raghunath Das Goswami, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, Gadhadhar Pandit, Pundarik Vidyanidhi, Viswamithra muni, King Dashratha Palimara Swami, Narada Muni, Maharaj Yudhistr

Tags – Themes

Renunciation, attachment to Krsna.

vārtā vicitrā śālīna-

yāyāvara-śiloṣchanam

vipra-vṛttiś caturdheyaṁ

śreyasī cottarottarā

[SB 7.11.16]

[As an alternative, a brāhmaṇa may also take to the vaiśya’s occupational duty of agriculture, cow protection, or trade. He may depend on that which he has received without begging, he may beg, in the paddy field every day, he may collect paddy left in a field by its proprietor, or he may collect food grains left here and there in the shops of grain dealers. These are four means of livelihood that may also be adopted by brāhmaṇas. Among these four, each of them in succession is better than the one preceding it.]

Purport

A brāhmaṇa is sometimes offered land and cows in charity, and thus for his livelihood he may act in the same way as a vaiśya, by cultivating land, giving protection to cows and trading off his surpluses. A better process, however, is to pick up grains from a field or from a dealer’s shop without begging.

Lecture Transcription

Standard of success in modern society

Here we are given an example of the standard of progress in a civilized culture based on spiritual principles. It is quite the opposite of the standard of success that we find in our modern society which is simply based on sense gratification. You see, amongst those materialistic persons, one who is successful according to the degree of sense gratification, he is able to enjoy. A person has great wealth or high education or great power and influence. This will facilitate a much greater degree of sense gratification than one can enjoy in life. So, therefore these things are considered very exalted. People are very envious of one another in the society when somebody else has more wealth, more beauty, higher education, better birth or in other words more facility for sense gratification. Just like, everyone in the world must have heard of Mr. Rockefeller. He is considered a very, very respected, and successful man in this world, because he has so much money.

Standard of success for a saintly person

But here we find that, amongst those who have proper intelligence, they honor and respect a person according to the degree that person is detached from sense gratification, is able and willing to live without the things that provide sense gratification. When a sadhu sees a wealthy man enjoying his riches, the people of this world are very envious, they all want to be like him. But the sadhu pities that person. He sees that the only wealth is bhakti. There is no other wealth. Everything else is simply disturbance if it is not helping in the progress of our bhakti. And to the degree one can live a simple life without these articles for sense gratification, to that degree that person is considered advanced in civilization.

Raghunath Das Goswami’s example

Just like, of course, we have the story of Raghunath Das Goswami. His father was most wealthy, and he had a beautiful wife and he had so much respect and prestige within the society. But he was willing to give it all up, for what? To live the most simple and austere life that anyone has ever seen. Raghunath Das Goswami was living in a very big mansion with the finest cooks, the finest servants, all the luxuries that everyone is striving and working so hard for. And he had to make…..he had to undergo a tremendously difficult endeavor to get away from it. His parents were forcing sense gratification upon him. And just the thought that he would leave all his sense gratification was breaking the heart of his mother and father.

Recently someone was telling us that you can't make spiritual advancement unless you take a path that gets the full blessings of your mother. Of course, if your mother is a transcendentalist that is true. But in the case of Raghunath Das Goswami, he completely shattered the hearts of his mother and father. He was a married man. And he was the only son, and his father had a very very big business worth billions and billions of rupees. And he was completely depending on Raghunath alone to take over everything. He educated Raghunath with the finest education, with the finest culture and here Raghunath was willing to leave all behind. His parents were so much disturbed. In fact, they were so much disturbed that they had guards, eleven guards constantly watching him to make sure that he did not escape. But by the arrangement of the Supreme God, Sri Caitanya, he was able to get away. Ah…

And he went to Jagannath Puri. And there was nothing his parents could do, except cry. So, what was happening is, his parents started sending money to support him in Jagannath Puri. But he would not even accept that money. First, he was just living a simple life, when he was taking that money and sponsoring nice feasts for other devotees. But then he wouldn't even do that. He was just sitting like a beggar in front of the Simha dwar gate at the Jagannath temple. And whoever would give him some alms, some donation, or foodstuffs, he would accept it. He became a beggar.

And then he was thinking that even a beggar does too much sense gratification. Ah…So, what he would do, is he would, the shopkeepers after they would finish selling all the prasad of Lord Jagannath, whatever was left at the end of the day, they would feed that rice to the cows in a certain place where the cows would gather. And the cows would eat that rice throughout the day. And at the end of the day, the particles of rice that were not…they were stale and were inedible to the cows, that they spit out or left behind. Raghunath at night, nobody would see him, he would come and collect those grains of rice and eat that once a day.

You notice, he did not do it so that he would become famous to be renounced because nobody even knew he was doing it. He would do it secretly. You see, sense gratification, there are many levels. Physical sense gratification is difficult to give up, but subtle sense gratification is even more difficult. Sometimes we find people who do very severe fasts, but they are very happy when everyone knows about their fasting. Because although physically they are torturing themselves, their false ego is being nourished for a huge feast of glorification and praise. But Raghunath Das Goswami was utterly detached from any desire from sense gratification at any level. He would come at night and secretly take this food.

Caitanya Mahaprabhu being all pervasive, he could understand. And Sri Caitanya Mahaprabhu when he went to Raghunath Das Goswami's house, he said, "Raghunath what are you eating?" He came just at the time Raghunath was about to take his Prasad. Raghunath did not want to tell Mahaprabhu but Caitanya Mahaprabhu knew exactly where his bowl was and grabbed it from him and he began to eat it himself. Swaroop Damodar said, "My Dear Lord, you should not eat this decomposed rice, you already had enough, you had one portion. That is enough to satisfy the universe". And Lord Caitanya said, "Ah, you know I have tasted it. I have tasted so much nice Prasad in my life. I have tasted all the varieties of Jagannath Prasad - sweetmeats, Sabjis, rice, so many different forms, but I have never ever, ever tasted anything so satisfying as that Prasad which Raghunath Das is eating every day. Raghunath, why don't you invite me to have Prasad with you? Why are you such a miser? You are keeping such opulent, luxurious dishes only for yourself.”

Because,

mahāprabhura bhakta-gaṇera vairāgya pradhāna
yāhā dekhi’ prīta hana gaura-bhagavān

[CC Antya 6.220]

[Renunciation is the basic principle sustaining the lives of Śrī Caitanya Mahāprabhu’s devotees. Seeing this renunciation, Śrī Caitanya Mahāprabhu, the Supreme Personality of Godhead, is extremely satisfied.]

Caitanya Mahaprabhu sees the devotion within his devotee. And Raghunath Das, he was…..he is, praised and glorified by all sincere transcendentalists. He is praised for the degree in which he lived a simple life giving up sense gratification. Now if a gross materialist would hear the story about Raghunath Das Goswami, they would think he is insane. In the brahmachari ashram, we are hoping every brahmachari will become like Raghunath Das Goswami. But your mother and father, they do not want you to become like Raghunath Das Goswami. They want you to become like Mr. Birla or someone like that, very successful and wealthy in society. So, this is the difference between material and spiritual consciousness.

Attached to sense gratification or to Krsna?

Because to the degree we give up sense gratification, to that exact degree we can fix our consciousness on Krishna. And to the degree we are attached to sense gratification, we cannot fix our consciousness on Krishna. Our heart is filled with the attachments for material pleasure. Therefore, it is said in Bhagavad Gita,

bhogaiśvarya-prasaktānāṁ

tayāpahṛta-cetasām

vyavasāyātmikā buddhiḥ

samādhau na vidhīyate

[BG 2.44]

[In the minds of those who are too attached to sense enjoyment and material opulence, and who are bewildered by such things, the resolute determination for devotional service to the Supreme Lord does not take place.]

For those who are too much attached to material opulence and sense enjoyment, the resolute determination for bhakti or devotion cannot take place. Of course, it is impossible for anyone in this day and age to come to the level of Raghunath Das Goswami. He is a nitya siddha, maha bhagwat. We cannot try to imitate him. If we try to imitate such an exalted platform of renunciation, we are certain to fall down from our religious life. But we can understand that this is the standard which we should always keep before us. And we should aspire to follow in his footsteps. Whether one is a brahmachari, a sanyasi, a vanaprastha or a grihastha, we should understand, the standard of our real success in life is according to the degree we give up our sense gratification, and we detach ourselves from those objects which provide sense gratification. And with that energy that is generally attaching itself to the objects of the senses, we must dovetail it in attaching ourselves to the service of God. Krishna is the enjoyer.

Why spend so much money on Krsna?

Yesterday afternoon we had the good fortune of visiting one of the prominent acaryas of Madhvacarya's sampradaya. In fact, he is the senior of all the sanyasis in the Madhva sampradaya. He is known by the name of Palimara Swami. He is about 76 years old. Now this man, he took sanyas when he was only 12 years old. And he has been….he lives very simple life, just wears very simple cloth, sits/sleeps on floor, eats very simple food, has no wife, no family. He has no sense enjoyment. Now people work very hard to raise money for sense gratification. Palimar Swami is in the process of spending great amounts of his energy to raise funds to buy a crown for Lord Sri Krishna. Now this crown, its cost is one crore of rupees that is almost one million dollars for one crown. It is a crown made out of diamonds.

Someone challenged, why don't you feed the poor with this one crore rupees, why don't you build hospitals with one crore rupees? Why are you spending all this money to build a crown for Krishna. He said, The President of U.S., the Prime Minister of India, the Prime Minister of England, every time they go somewhere they have a private jet, they have secret service men protecting them, they are put in very very high security, very luxurious residence wherever they go. Do you know how much money this cost? They are spending crores of rupees, crores & crores & crores of Rupees every year for practically every Chief of State in the world. All the facilities that they try to give him, all the honor, prestige. And these people are just kings of just a tiny little track of land. And yet who's saying why they are spending crores & crores & crores of rupees to facilitate Rajiv Gandhi or George Bush. Nobody says that, because they expect that he is the PM, he is the King. He should have the best facilities.

But Krishna, he is Jagat Pate. He is the King of the entire existence. He is the king of all the planets & all the universes and the entire creations and beyond. So, nobody questions when we spend Crores & Crores & Crores of Rs for some tiny, little, insignificant politician whose domain is some tiny little insignificant track of land. But when you want to give some nice gesture of glorification for the Supreme of all Kings, then people will…. But actually, if we are to buy a diamond crown for Krishna, every hour of the day, it would not be too much. It would be just a tiny little insignificant gesture of an offering to him.

After all, what do you think the diamonds in this world grow for? The diamonds are there, they are in the ground. The ordinary man has no objection to spend his hard-earned money to give it to his wife or put on his own finger, but if you put it on God everyone is saying, “why don’t you feed the poor?” You are feeding the poor. By watering the root of the tree, everyone is nourished.

I remember one time in New Vrindavan, one lady, not in New Vrindavan, but I was in Dayton, Ohio, giving a lecture. And after the lecture we were having prasad. And one lady who was a very wealthy woman. She saw picture of Prabhupada's Palace of Gold. Why have you spent so much money building this palace? Why don't you use this money to feed poor people, open hospitals & do good to the society? I asked what kind of car do you drive? She said a Thunderbird. Now a Thunderbird costs twenty thousand dollars. How much is that in rupees? About three lacs or four lakhs. So, I said, “Why don't you drive a used Ford? You can buy one for five hundred dollars, sell your Thunderbird & give fifteen thousand five hundred dollars to the poor. Why don't you do that?”

The fact is these devotees are working hard and they are feeding hundreds of poor people every day. And they are also enlightening people, the rich people like you are much poorer than the poor people, driving in your big Thunderbirds but spiritually you are poverty stricken. So, the devotees, factually they have nothing, they are sleeping on floors, they have nothing for themselves. They are dedicating all their lives to helping others in every way, but they know the most important part of life is glorifying God. So, everyone is quick to criticize when you do something for Krishna. But when you do something for your own sense gratification, everyone just accepts that - this is normal life, this is expected, this is how you should live.

Develop Higher Taste

So Palimar Swami. a very simple man, he doesn't have a single piece of jewelry on his own body but for Krishna -diamond crown. Prabhupada said that when you decorate Krishna beautifully you lose all desire to decorate yourself. Because you find so much more satisfaction because Krishna is the enjoyer. If you don't understand how beautiful and wonderful the taste is of decorating Krishna, then you will think "Oh! How enjoyable it is to decorate my own body." It is a higher taste. When we see the beauty of Krishna, it satisfies the soul.

When we look in the mirror and see the beauty of ourselves, it temporarily satisfies our senses, but then in a few years when you look in the mirror and see all your beauty is gone, then you become very frustrated and sad. Where is my beauty gone? Where is my youth gone? Where has my wealth gone? As you attach yourself to your own senses and the objects, you are simply creating a situation of suffering for the future, because it is temporary.

So therefore, here we are finding that the Brahmans who are the most respected people in society, who are in the highest state of the social ladder, they are the most materially impoverished. And even the great kings, they honor and worship and want to become like the Brahman. We read about people like Vishwamitra, when he was living in the forest, wearing only a simple deer skin, and when he came to Ayodhya, Dashratha because he was the King he had to accept on behalf of God, so many things, he had so many jewels and crown and big, big throne and beautiful wives and beautiful children, beautiful servants and wonderful living facilities, luxuries beyond our imagination. But when he saw Vishwamitra Muni, he bowed down to him, his head at Vishwamitra's feet. Because he honored that real renunciation is factually a greater opulence than all of my material facilities.

Real opulence is renunciation.

One Sanyasi, a very great Gaudiya Vaishnav, was telling me that real opulence is renunciation. That is the highest opulence for a devotee. He said, when you see a King with a crown and you see a Sanyasi with a danda, which is more opulent? The Sanyasi - this is real opulence. This is real beauty in the eyes of one who is transcendental in vision.

Real standard of success

So of course, this does not necessarily mean that we should all become Sanyasis. But we have to have our standards properly focused in life. We have to know that Bhakti, love, is the real standard of success and material sense gratification is the standard of degradation, whether you are a King living in the wealthiest conditions like Maharaja Yudhisthir, Maharaj Priyavrata, Maharaj Rishabhadev, they were great glorious kings, but they were not attached. They were attached to God. They were accepting those facilities only in their service to the Lord. You don't have to give away these things. But we have to give away our worldly attachment for sense gratification knowing that it is the greatest stumbling block and obstacle for our advancement in devotional service.

Position of Brahmana in society

And therefore, according to the Vedic civilization, the Brahmans accept the vow of poverty. They are not allowed according to the Sastras; a Brahman is not allowed to accept employment. You see Brahman is not by birth. Today, you see so many shopkeepers, they say I am Brahman. Really according to the Sastric plan, a Brahman should not accept employment. He should only live by; he should only be giving free of charge spiritual knowledge and spiritual direction. And whatever people give to him he accepts. And whatever is needed to maintain his body, he maintains and whatever is needed to maintain his preaching, he maintains and all the rest he gives for the benefit of others, either by publishing books or by distributing in charity in some way to uplift the spiritual consciousness of others.

In this way the whole society respects, adores and trusts the Brahman because they know that he is not trying to cheat them. They know he has nothing to gain. He is only giving; his only business is giving. And such a person in this condition of life, even the kings and the great crorepati vaishyas, they all bow their heads before him. They know he has the opulence I really want. And when people learn to respect and honour the renunciation of the sanyasis and brahmacharis who are truly in the spirit of compassion and preaching, to that degree the whole society advances spiritually.

Recently one of our members was saying that this brahmachari ashram is impractical. Really if you have a good job, you are a professional man and you preach like that, people will honor the integrity that you are a working man like them and still you are Krishna conscious, but nobody wants to be a brahmachari. So therefore, brahmachari and sanyasi is a useless ashram for preaching. Everyone should get married, should have a staunch professional image and at the same time preach. And people will honor and respect such persons.

Brahmanas set the right standards.

But according to Sastra, according to God that is very good. The grihasthas should all be like that, that is perfect. But the fact is that grihasthas cannot be like that unless there are those in the renounced order of life, setting a standard to show them the way. As I was saying, even great kings of the Vedic times, they were like that. They were perfectly Krishna conscious. They were professional men. They were responsible men but at the same time they were devotees, and they were preaching, and they were pushing forward the cause of God. But every one of those kings understood that they are completely depending on the examples and instructions of those in the renounced order of life to maintain the integrity of their spiritual consciousness. And that is a fact.

That is why there must be the majority of society grihasthas, who are God centered, who are detached from sense gratification but who are living within the society responsible in the service of Krishna. But there also absolutely must be the class of brahmacharis and sanyasis to maintain the pure standards by which the grihasthas can remain Krishna conscious. To the degree the grihasthas, even the wealthiest grihasthas, they don't have to give away their wealth, but the degree they honor and respect and adore those sadhus who are dedicating everything in the compassionate mission of uplifting others and who have given all the thing of this world, to that very degree they will advance in Krishna consciousness. But however very expert they may be, in whatever field of religion and materially progress, if they do not have that proper respect and appreciation for the renounced order, then their spiritual life will be checked.

Real opulence of a Brahmana

So that is why we find in this verse by Narada muni to Maharaj Yudhistir, the real opulence of the Brahman is his simplicity, his faith in God, his detachment from this material world and his attachment to the mission of the Lord. And whatever walk of life we may be in, we must really try to cultivate within our heart the aspiration for this, as the goal of life. Because you know the tendency in this society is, even amongst devotees, we become illusioned by the phantasmagoria of the glitter of this material world and we start thinking, “Oh! This person has a better house, this person has a nice car, this person has a big position, this person is very influential. I would like to be like that. I would like to have that.” Or even sometimes we think having these things is a sign of advancement in spiritual life. And then we start to aspire for these things, rather than aspire for Krishna. We should not fall under this illusion. We should know very clearly what opulence is, what is wealth? Wealth is renunciation. Renunciation means no attachment, a simple life attached to Krishna.

Gadadhar Pandit meets Pundarik Vidyanidhi

There is a story of Gadhadhar Pandit when he first met Pundarik Vidyanidhi. Pundarik Vidyanidhi was a very wealthy man. And he had so much glamour around him. Gadhadhar Pandit was thinking, “Oh! This man is very materialistic, what kind of devotee is he?” But then one day when someone was reciting Krishna's leela, Pundarik Vidyanidhi went into ecstatic symptoms, it wasn't a show. It was genuine from the heart, Gadhadhar Pandit could see, he was crying, he was rolling, he was destroying everything in his house, he was in such an ecstasy. And Gadhadhar was thinking, “My God! Although his responsibility in this world is to live in this environment, he is really, completely detached. He is really a great saint; he has such attachment for Krishna.” And Gadhadhar Pandit who was a sanyasi, he accepted Pundarik Vidyanidhi as his guru, who was a worldly man, apparently with all riches and many responsibilities of this world and a family and everything.

Real wealth – attachment to Krsna.

So, it is not a question of whether you have wealth, it is a question of whether you are attached to wealth or whether you are attached to Krishna. This is real wealth, attachment to Krishna. So, whether you are Pundarik Vidyanidhi or whether you are Raghunath Das Goswami, whether you give everything up and live the most severe, simple life of tapasya. It is said that when the parents heard how Raghunath Das was living, they simply cried. They could not do anything but cry. Whether you are living like that or whether you are living like Pundarik Vidyanidhi in all worldly opulence but completely detached and attached to Krishna, your life is perfect.

But we have to keep our focus very clear, that the standard of success, the standard of wealth is detachment from sense gratification, detachment from the facilities for sense gratification and the real wealth is bhakti - devotion. One who takes pleasure in the chanting of the holy name, that person is the wealthiest man on earth. But one who cannot take pleasure in the chanting of the holy name, even if he is living in a castle, we should cry for that person, we should pity that person, because he has nothing. This must be our conviction.

Thank you very much. Hare Krishna!

Q&A Transcription

Q. Renunciation is to give up all you have. So how can the devotee know what to give up?

A. Renunciation means to give up the false sense of proprietorship. You have nothing. Everything is the property of Krishna.

"Sarva loka maheshvaram"

[BG 5.29]

Nothing is yours. The only thing you have to renounce is the false conception that you have something to renounce. Everything is the property of God. Correct? So real renunciation according to Rupa Goswami is,

prāpaṣcikatayā buddhyā

hari-sambandhi-vastunaḥ

mumukṣubhiḥ parityāgo

vairāgyaṁ phalgu kathyate

[Bhakti-rasāmṛta-sindhu 1.2.258]

[By development of Kṛṣṇa consciousness one can know that everything has its use in the service of the Lord. Those who are without knowledge of Kṛṣṇa consciousness artificially try to avoid material objects, and as a result, although they desire liberation from material bondage, they do not attain to the perfect stage of renunciation. Their so-called renunciation is called phalgu, or less important.]

Not to give up the things you have but to use it for Krishna's pleasure. Use them in the service of Krishna rather than in the service of your senses. That is the highest renunciation. So we are not concerned with the English translation of renunciation, we are concerned with the explanation of renunciation given by the acaryas. And Rupa Goswami has described what is real renunciation. To use everything in the service of Krishna. To use nothing for one's own personal selfish interests.

Thank you very much. Hare Krishna!
